name: Phosgène
description: 2HP digital wavetable/FM oscillator. Two banks of 30 wavetables run in parallel with separate outputs, 100Hz FM base, V/Oct tracking, octave and octave-displace switching, and stored bank/wave/octave settings.

controls.name: Bank
controls.description: Selects between the two wavetable banks.

controls.name: Wave
controls.description: Chooses the wavetable within the selected bank.

controls.name: Octave Displace
controls.description: Shifts wavetable playback down the octave scale without affecting the FM path.

inputs.name: Bank CV
inputs.description: External modulation of the wavetable bank.

inputs.name: Wave CV
inputs.description: External modulation of the wavetable selection.

inputs.name: FM CV
inputs.description: External modulation of the FM path.

behaviors.name: Parallel oscillator paths
behaviors.description: Phosgène exposes wavetable and FM in parallel. That makes it easy to split a patch into a harmonically stable path and a harsher FM path without needing a second oscillator.

behaviors.name: 100Hz FM base
behaviors.description: The FM path uses a 100Hz base. The result is a darker, more controlled modulation spectrum that stays useful for basses and low, metallic sidebands.

behaviors.name: Octave displace
behaviors.description: The octave-displace function only applies to the wavetable path. FM keeps the full range, so the same pitch gesture can drive low folded tables while leaving FM wide open.

behaviors.name: Stored startup state
behaviors.description: The module recalls the last bank, wave and octave settings after reboot. That makes it behave like a preset oscillator even though it is still a hands-on 2HP voice.

behaviors.name: High-octave alias texture
behaviors.description: The 11-bit wavetable engine and high octave range can produce deliberate digital trash at the top end. That makes it suitable for brittle leads, percussion and noisy texture.

patchIdeas.name: Parallel voice split
patchIdeas.patch: WT OUT to a filter and FM OUT to a second voice or distortion path · one oscillator becomes both the clean source and the aggressive source at once.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Bank: 1
* Wave: mid
* Octave: 0
* Octave Displace: off

Filter:
Distortion:
Mixer:

- Phosgène (WT OUT) -> Filter (In)
- Phosgène (FM OUT) -> Distortion (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Distortion (Out) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Dark FM bass
patchIdeas.patch: FM CV pushed from a sequencer or envelope · 100Hz FM base keeps the modulation under control · WT OUT can stay low while FM OUT carries the bite.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Bank: 1
* Wave: bass
* Octave: -1
* Octave Displace: off

Envelope:
VCA:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Phosgène (V/Oct)
- Envelope (Out) >> Phosgène (FM CV)
- Phosgène (FM OUT) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Low drone with displaced tables
patchIdeas.patch: Octave Displace on · a slowly moving wavetable bank feeds the main output · keep FM low or unused for a stable low drone with bright aliasing edges when you open the octave switch.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Bank: 2
* Wave: 12
* Octave: -2
* Octave Displace: on

Filter:
Output:

- Phosgène (WT OUT) -> Filter (In)
- Phosgène (FM OUT) -> Output (Spare)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Glitch percussion voice
patchIdeas.patch: Fast pitch steps into V/Oct · rapid wave changes on the wavetable CV input · the top end folds into brittle digital percussion while FM adds an independent metallic accent.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Phosgène:
* Bank: 1
* Wave: 7
* Octave: 1
* Octave Displace: off

Clock:
VCA:
Output:

- Clock (Out) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Phosgène (V/Oct)
- Sequencer (CV) >> Phosgène (Wave CV)
- Phosgène (WT OUT) -> VCA (In)
- Clock (Out) t> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)
